summary: Verified the ClamAV concurrency-cap OOM fix in /app/backend/utils/antivirus.py. Ran the two target tests in tests/test_antivirus_concurrency.py (both PASS), then a broader antivirus-related sweep (tests/test_iter77_av_background.py + tests/test_av_gate_friend_library.py + tests/test_av_watchdog.py) — all PASS. Added a small regression suite tests/test_antivirus_regression.py (5 tests) pinning the ScanResult shape (ok, infected, signature, scanner, elapsed_ms, error) and fail-open behavior via scan_bytes()/scan_path() when the scanner is unavailable, empty input, missing file, and oversize input — all PASS. Backend /api/health returns 200 with antivirus.ok=true and mongo.ok=true, confirming the antivirus.py refactor didn't break import/boot.
critical_code_review_comments: utils/antivirus.py: BoundedSemaphore is initialised at module import from AV_MAX_CONCURRENT_SCANS. Operators changing the env var require a backend restart to take effect; consider exposing a private setter or reading the env at first use if runtime tuning is ever needed (minor — current behaviour matches K8s deploy conventions).

The 30s acquire timeout in _run_clamscan returns ok=False with a friendly error but callers currently treat 'antivirus unavailable' identically (fail-open); if queue-full ever becomes common under load, consider a distinct status/metric so operators can tell 'saturated' apart from 'scanner missing'.

test_env_var_tunes_the_cap only asserts _AV_MAX_CONCURRENT >= 1 and equals the env default OR 2 — it doesn't actually re-import with a different value, so it's more of a smoke check than a true tuning test. Left as-is per the file's docstring caveat; a stronger test could importlib.reload(antivirus) under monkeypatched env, but that risks cross-test state leak with the module-level semaphore.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_antivirus_regression.py (new — 5 regression tests for ScanResult shape + fail-open)

success_rate.backend: 100% (11/11 antivirus-scoped tests passed; unrelated test_av_fields.py had 5 HTTP-login timeouts flagged as known-flaky by main agent)

test_credentials: n/a — not required for this verification
context_for_next_testing_agent: AV concurrency cap fix verified end-to-end: (1) both tests in tests/test_antivirus_concurrency.py pass in ~0.9s, (2) added tests/test_antivirus_regression.py (5 tests) pinning ScanResult shape + fail-open, (3) tests/test_iter77_av_background.py (4 tests) still green, (4) /api/health returns 200 with antivirus.ok=true. Known-flaky tests/test_av_fields.py (HTTP /api/auth/login timeouts) are unrelated to antivirus.py — main agent should keep those in the known-flaky-HTTP bucket. If runtime tuning of AV_MAX_CONCURRENT_SCANS is ever needed, note the semaphore is created at module import so a backend restart is required after changing the env var.
rca of the issue: n/a — verification pass only; no regressions found. The OOM fix (BoundedSemaphore capped at AV_MAX_CONCURRENT_SCANS=2 gating _run_clamscan) is in place at /app/backend/utils/antivirus.py:62-129, and the burst test proves at most 2 scans run concurrently under an 8-thread burst while all 8 complete within 5s.
